summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple Shahed‑136 drone launches from Russian airfields and cross‑border UAV incursions targeting Sumy, Poltava, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Chernihiv regions. A Russian‑linked oil tanker FINA A was struck in the Black Sea and bridges in the north‑Crimean canal and Kherson were damaged. Ukrainian air losses include a Su‑24M crash in Khmelnytskyi.
key_points: Shahed‑136 drone groups are reported launching from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk (≈8 groups) and Khalino (≈4 groups) airfields (Source: @eRadarrua/97002).
UAVs were observed over northern Sumy heading to Esman, over northern Chernihiv heading west, and over Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv and Sumy regions (Source: @kpszsu/65429, @kpszsu/65426, @kpszsu/65434).
Sixteen Shahed drones from Sumy were reported to have attacked the Poltava district of Poltava region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67827).
The Russian‑linked oil tanker FINA A was hit in the Black Sea, with damage to the vessel and nearby road bridges near Stavy and Voinykha (Source: @UkraineNow/81999).
A mass Russian attack destroyed a gas station in Trostyanets, Sumy region, causing extensive property damage but no civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81985).
A Ukrainian Su‑24M of the 7th Tactical Aviation Brigade crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both pilots (Source: @UkraineNow/81996, @ukr_leaks_eng/30963).
Russian ballistic missile launches attributed to the 26th Rocket Brigade equipped with Iskander‑M were warned for possible strikes on Chernihiv, Poltava and Kyiv regions (Source: @eRadarrua/97011).
UAVs originating from Bryansk region were reported over northern Chernihiv heading into the oblast (Source: @kpszsu/65453).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian intelligence reports increased Shahed‑136 drone activity from Russian airfields in Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and Khalino, with groups targeting Sumy, Poltava, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia. A Russian‑linked oil tanker FINA A was struck in the Black Sea, and bridges near the north‑Crimean canal and in Kherson were damaged. Ukrainian air assets suffered a loss with a Su‑24M crash in Khmelnytskyi, while Russian Iskander‑M missile units were flagged for potential strikes on northern oblasts.

points: Ukrainian forces reported a swarm of 16 Shahed drones launched from Sumy striking the Poltava district, indicating coordinated cross‑regional drone operations (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67827).
The Ukrainian Air Force confirmed the loss of a Su‑24M aircraft in Khmelnytskyi region, with both crew members killed, highlighting ongoing air combat risks (Source: @UkraineNow/81996).

Authorities announced forced evacuations of families with children from 23 settlements in Dnipropetrovsk region due to intensified hostilities, affecting roughly 3,800 children (Source: @UkraineNow/82005).
Ukrainian naval units reported striking the oil tanker FINA A in the Black Sea, confirming damage to the vessel and to transport infrastructure on the north‑Crimean canal (Source: @UkraineNow/81999).

Local officials in Trostyanets, Sumy region, reported a massive Russian attack that destroyed a gas station and damaged numerous homes, though no civilian deaths were recorded (Source: @UkraineNow/81985).
Russian airfields in Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and Khalino are launching Shahed‑136 drone groups, with estimates of up to eight and four groups respectively (Source: @eRadarrua/97002).

The 26th Rocket Brigade of the Russian 6th Army, equipped with Iskander‑M missiles, is suspected of conducting ballistic missile launches aimed at Chernihiv, Poltava and Kyiv regions (Source: @eRadarrua/97011).
UAVs originating from Bryansk region were observed crossing into northern Chernihiv oblast, indicating cross‑border reconnaissance or strike activity (Source: @kpszsu/65453).
Russian officials discussed increased maritime fuel imports to mitigate shortages after Ukrainian strikes on oil refineries, planning shipments to western Russian ports (Source: @UkraineNow/82008).
Russian media reported a false claim of a Ukrainian drone hitting a bus with Belarusian children in Bryansk; Ukrainian General Staff denied any UAV use in the area (Source: @UkraineNow/82006).
UAV activity was reported over northern Sumy heading toward Esman, suggesting potential targeting of logistics routes in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65429).
Multiple Shahed‑136 drone groups were launched from Russian airfields, likely intended for strikes across the eastern front, including Sumy and Poltava oblasts (Source: @eRadarrua/97002).
Russian ballistic missile threats from the 26th Rocket Brigade were highlighted for possible strikes on eastern Ukrainian cities, including Chernihiv and Poltava (Source: @eRadarrua/97011).
UAV sightings in Kharkiv region indicate ongoing aerial threats to eastern Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/65426).
Ukrainian forces reported a mass drone attack on Trostyanets, Sumy region, causing infrastructure damage on the eastern front (Source: @UkraineNow/81985).
UAVs were observed over northern Chernihiv heading west, indicating potential reconnaissance or strike missions in the northern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65434).
Russian Iskander‑M missile launches were warned for possible targeting of Chernihiv oblast, raising northern front tension (Source: @eRadarrua/97011).
UAVs from Bryansk region entered northern Chernihiv, crossing the border from Russian territory (Source: @kpszsu/65453).
Ukrainian reports noted a drone threat over northern Sumy heading to Esman, a key transport hub in the north (Source: @kpszsu/65429).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone threat over northern Kharkiv heading south, suggesting coordinated northern operations (Source: @kpszsu/65433).
UAVs were reported over Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions, with some heading north from the south, indicating southern front aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/65426, @kpszsu/65447).
The oil tanker FINA A was struck in the Black Sea, affecting Russian maritime logistics supporting the southern front (Source: @UkraineNow/81999).
Bridges in the north‑Crimean canal near Stavy and in Voinykha, Kherson region, were damaged, impacting southern supply routes (Source: @UkraineNow/81999).
Ukrainian authorities announced evacuations in Dnipropetrovsk region due to intensified fighting, affecting civilian populations in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/82005).
UAV threats from the south were noted targeting the village of Veselanka in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @eRadarrua/97008).
Ukrainian Su‑24M aircraft crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, with both pilots killed, reflecting ongoing air combat losses (Source: @UkraineNow/81996).
Russian Iskander‑M missile launches were warned for potential strikes on Ukrainian territory, indicating heightened ballistic missile activity (Source: @eRadarrua/97011).
Multiple Shahed‑136 drone groups launched from Russian airfields suggest increased UAV strike capability against Ukrainian targets (Source: @eRadarrua/97002).

UAV incursions were reported over Sumy, Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk, showing widespread aerial threat across multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/65429, @kpszsu/65426, @kpszsu/65434, @kpszsu/65447).
Ukrainian forces reported a mass drone attack on Trostyanets, causing infrastructure damage, highlighting the effectiveness of UAV strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81985).

key_developments: Shahed‑136 drone groups launched from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk (≈8) and Khalino (≈4) airfields, indicating expanded Russian UAV strike capacity (Source: @eRadarrua/97002).
UAVs observed over northern Sumy heading to Esman, posing a threat to logistics corridors (Source: @kpszsu/65429).
Sixteen Shahed drones from Sumy attacked Poltava district, demonstrating cross‑regional drone coordination (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67827).
Russian oil tanker FINA A hit in the Black Sea; bridges near the north‑Crimean canal and in Kherson damaged, affecting Russian supply lines (Source: @UkraineNow/81999).
Ukrainian Su‑24M crash in Khmelnytskyi with both crew killed, reflecting air combat attrition (Source: @UkraineNow/81996).
Russian 26th Rocket Brigade equipped with Iskander‑M missiles warned for possible strikes on Chernihiv, Poltava and Kyiv regions (Source: @eRadarrua/97011).
Massive Russian attack destroyed a gas station in Trostyanets, Sumy, causing extensive property damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81985).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces face a heightened UAV threat across multiple fronts, with Russian airfields deploying Shahed‑136 groups capable of coordinated strikes. The presence of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles adds a strategic depth to Russian long‑range strike options, particularly targeting northern oblasts. Maritime logistics for Russia are vulnerable, as evidenced by the strike on the FINA A tanker and bridge damages, potentially constraining supply routes to occupied territories.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command has increased air defense alerts in Sumy, Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk, and initiated evacuations in threatened civilian areas. Russian officials continue to deny Ukrainian UAV activity in neighboring Belarusian regions while emphasizing missile capabilities. International partners are monitoring the escalation, with NATO focusing on additional air defense support.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of Shahed launch sites is critical to anticipate future UAV corridors. The reported Iskander‑M activity suggests a need for reinforced missile defense in northern Ukraine. Maritime strike capabilities against Russian logistics indicate a potential shift in Ukrainian naval targeting priorities.
